summary.intent: Implement GridList adapter components for both Leptos and Dioxus frameworks in the ars-ui Rust UI library (tasks #398 and #490), with iterative UX improvements to match React Aria's reference implementation, improved adapter-level documentation, and architectural refactoring to move shared behavior into the agnostic core layer.
summary.outcome: GridList adapters were implemented for both Leptos and Dioxus with selection, DnD (including grouped drag, live preview, and persisted reorder), and core-driven render state. A docs-only PR (#721) was opened and iterated through multiple Codex review cycles. The implementation code remains uncommitted pending user approval and a final `cargo xci-fast` run.

repo: Adapter test files must be named `test_*.rs` (e.g., `test_grid_list.rs`) not `grid_list.rs` for the parity lint to discover them.
The repo uses `cargo xtask` for custom commands: `e2e`, `lint adapter-parity`, `spec validate`, etc.
E2E fixtures use directory-backed category modules: `categories/data_display/grid_list.rs` not `categories/data_display.rs`.
`cargo xci-fast` must be run before final commit/push per repo workflow.
Adapter components must not claim 'full parity' in specs unless a parity matrix proves it.
The agnostic core (`crates/ars-components`) must own all renderer-independent behavior (selection, DnD, layout, loading suppression).
Examples and demos must NOT reimplement component behavior — they should only hold demo state and apply emitted callbacks.
Widget examples must visually match the reference implementation (React Aria > Ark UI > Radix > others).
Browser visual verification must happen before reporting completion on UX work.
The docs were split from one large `adapter-component-delivery.md` into modular files under `docs/implementation/adapter-components/`.

code.finding: Leptos wasm tests require `Arc<Mutex<_>>` instead of `Rc<RefCell<_>>` due to Send+Sync bounds on callbacks.
Dioxus `EventHandler` is not Send+Sync and cannot be captured inside agnostic `Callback` props. Dioxus render callbacks must also explicitly return `Element` type.

Drop completion in DnD must use core's pending drop target (not re-derive from the DOM event), and render callbacks should receive `ItemRenderState` (including selected/drag/drop state from core) not raw `ItemDef`.
Leptos block rendering must be wrapped in reactive closures for DnD state to update cells correctly; static spread of attrs is insufficient for dynamic state.
E2E fixtures use directory-backed category modules; adding a new component requires creating `categories/<category>/<component>.rs` and registering it in the module tree.

workflow: Pre-commit hooks run silently for minutes on every commit — monitor git log to verify completion rather than assuming a hung process.
In-app browser automation cannot emit HTML drag events via pointer/coordinate methods; real Playwright/WebDriver E2E is required for DnD testing.
Sandbox blocks localhost server binding, requiring escalation for local dev servers.
`dx serve` dev server frequently shows a stale rebuild overlay in browser tabs — hard-refresh or restart may be needed.
Codex review thread resolution requires GraphQL thread IDs, not comment database IDs; the fetch_comments.py script provides these.
GridList DnD grouped drag, live preview, and persisted reorder all required multiple implementation iterations — plan for several cycles on DnD features.

friction: Pre-commit hooks run silently for minutes with no progress indicator, making it unclear if the process is stuck.
Browser automation cannot emit HTML drag events via pointer/coordinate methods, blocking full in-app DnD verification.
Sandbox environment blocks localhost server binding, preventing local dev server usage without escalation.
`dx serve` dev server frequently shows stale rebuild overlays, requiring manual intervention to confirm changes.
Codex review thread resolution requires GraphQL thread IDs rather than the more accessible comment database IDs, adding friction to PR iteration.
Dioxus `EventHandler` not being Send+Sync required architectural workarounds to avoid capturing it in agnostic core callbacks.
DnD correctness required multiple implementation iterations — grouped drag, live preview, and persisted reorder each had distinct bugs.
open_items: Checkbox adapter implementation explicitly deferred to a separate session/branch.
GridList implementation PR code is uncommitted — pending user approval and a passing `cargo xci-fast` run before commit/push.
Full E2E drag/drop simulation via synthetic events is still imperfect due to complex timing and re-render issues.
GridList parity features still partially missing vs React Aria: sections, full DnD API, keyboard DnD, and layout/orientation props are partial implementations.
Docs PR #721 is still open awaiting final Codex review approval after the last fix commit.
GridList `dioxus-web` dependency added under the `web` feature — should be audited to confirm it does not affect non-browser builds.
